mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in months with rising PAYEMS does not exceed 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding fifty basis points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating labor demand and falling household income, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency when the trigger fires is below fifty-five percent over any three-year rolling window, or the mean twenty-day return in triggered months is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate combined with contracting industrial production indicates restrictive monetary policy is suppressing real output, a joint condition that raises recession probability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a bivariate regression, or the sixty-day return is positive in more than half of the joint-signal observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, an accelerating pace of retail sales growth signals reviving consumer demand and inventory rebuilding, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency during triggered post-recession windows is below fifty-five percent, or the mean twenty-day return in those windows is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and operating leverage, which boosts earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency in weeks with rising INDPRO is below fifty-five percent, or the Rank IC computed over any two-year rolling window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained above five percent creates restrictive liquidity conditions that raise debt-service burdens and compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent is below fifty-five percent, or the sixty-day mean return in those periods is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts reveal broad-based weakness in consumer spending and residential investment, a joint signal that forecasts sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative returns less than fifty-five percent of the time, or the interaction term does not improve prediction R-squared relative to the sum of individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already elevated above seven percent, a further decline in nonfarm payrolls signals deepening labor-market slack and triggers broad de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency when the trigger fires is below fifty-five percent, or the mean five-day return in high-unemployment payroll-decline months is not significantly below zero.
